member.name: M:FCQRS.SubscribeExtensions.SubscribeForFirst``1(FCQRS.Query.ISubscribe{``0},FCQRS.Model.Data.CID,System.Func{``0,System.Boolean})
member.summary: Subscribe for the first event matching the correlation id and a filter.

member.name: M:FCQRS.SubscribeExtensions.SubscribeForFirst``1(FCQRS.Query.ISubscribe{``0},FCQRS.Model.Data.CID)
member.summary: Subscribe for the first event matching the correlation id (take = 1).

member.name: M:FCQRS.SubscribeExtensions.SubscribeFor``1(FCQRS.Query.ISubscribe{``0},System.Func{``0,System.Boolean},System.Int32)
member.summary: Subscribe for `take` events matching a filter.

member.name: M:FCQRS.SubscribeExtensions.SubscribeFor``1(FCQRS.Query.ISubscribe{``0},FCQRS.Model.Data.CID,System.Func{``0,System.Boolean},System.Int32)
member.summary: Subscribe for `take` events matching the correlation id and a filter.

member.name: M:FCQRS.SubscribeExtensions.SubscribeFor``1(FCQRS.Query.ISubscribe{``0},FCQRS.Model.Data.CID,System.Int32)
member.summary: Subscribe for `take` events matching the correlation id.

member.name: T:FCQRS.SubscribeExtensions
member.summary: Top-level C# extension methods for ISubscribe. These live at *namespace* level (not nested inside the FCQRS.CSharp module) so C# actually discovers them as extensions — consumers `using FCQRS;` and write `subs.SubscribeFor(cid, n)` / `subs.SubscribeForFirst(cid)`.

member.name: M:FCQRS.AsyncTaskExtensions.AsTask``1(Microsoft.FSharp.Control.FSharpAsync{``0})
member.summary: Run an F# Async as a Task (Async.StartAsTask).

member.name: T:FCQRS.AsyncTaskExtensions
member.summary: Top-level Async->Task conversion, discoverable from C# as `someAsync.AsTask()` (`using FCQRS;`). Replaces the non-discoverable FCQRS.CSharp.AsyncExtensions.

member.name: M:FCQRS.FcqrsServiceCollectionExtensions.AggregateFactory``1(System.IServiceProvider)
member.summary: Resolve a registered aggregate's entity-ref factory by its CLR type. Use inside a saga's `create` delegate to reference the aggregates it coordinates.

member.name: M:FCQRS.FcqrsServiceCollectionExtensions.AddFcqrs(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IServiceCollection,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Register a SQLite-backed FCQRS actor system and the startup wiring. Returns a builder for fluent .AddAggregate / .AddSaga / .AddProjection registration. IConfiguration and ILoggerFactory are taken from the container.

member.name: T:FCQRS.FcqrsServiceCollectionExtensions
member.summary: `services.AddFcqrs(...)` and `serviceProvider.Aggregate<T>()`.

member.name: T:FCQRS.FcqrsHostedService
member.summary: The single startup step: creates the actor system (via the IActor singleton), runs the recorded registration steps in order, wires the saga-starter from all registered sagas, and starts the projection. Stops the actor system on shutdown.

member.name: P:FCQRS.FcqrsBuilder.Services
member.summary: The underlying service collection (so you can keep chaining .Add… on it).

member.name: M:FCQRS.FcqrsBuilder.AddSaga``4(System.Func{System.IServiceProvider,``0},System.Func{System.Object,System.Boolean})

M:FCQRS.FcqrsBuilder.AddProjection(System.Func{System.IServiceProvider,System.Func{System.Int64,System.Object,System.Collections.Generic.IList{FCQRS.Model.Data.IMessageWithCID}}},System.Func{System.IServiceProvider,System.Int32})
member.summary: Register the read-model projection, building the handler (and resuming offset) from DI. Use this overload when the projection needs services — e.g. an ILoggerFactory — so it resolves them the same way the actor system does.

member.name: M:FCQRS.FcqrsBuilder.AddProjection(System.Func{System.Int64,System.Object,System.Collections.Generic.IList{FCQRS.Model.Data.IMessageWithCID}},System.Int32)
member.summary: Register the read-model projection, resuming from the given offset (default 0).

member.name: M:FCQRS.FcqrsBuilder.AddAggregate``4
member.summary: Register an aggregate. The shard is constructed via DI (ctor args resolved from the container) and Init'd at startup; its Handler and AggregateRefs are registered so endpoints/sagas can resolve them.

member.name: T:FCQRS.FcqrsBuilder
member.summary: Fluent registration builder. Each AddXxx records a step to run at startup and, where relevant, registers the resolved piece (Handler, refs, subscription) in DI. Returned by IServiceCollection.AddFcqrs.

member.name: F:FCQRS.FcqrsRuntime.Subscription@
member.summary: The projection subscription, set when the projection step runs at startup.

member.name: P:FCQRS.FcqrsRuntime.Subscription(FCQRS.Query.ISubscribe)
member.summary: The projection subscription, set when the projection step runs at startup.

member.name: P:FCQRS.FcqrsRuntime.Subscription
member.summary: The projection subscription, set when the projection step runs at startup.

member.name: P:FCQRS.FcqrsRuntime.Actor
member.summary: The live actor system.

member.name: M:FCQRS.FcqrsRuntime.Register(System.Type,FCQRS.CSharp.AggregateFactory,System.Object)
member.summary: Record an aggregate's wiring under its CLR type (called at startup).

member.name: M:FCQRS.FcqrsRuntime.Refs``2(System.Type)
member.summary: The typed refs of a registered aggregate.

member.name: M:FCQRS.FcqrsRuntime.Factory(System.Type)
member.summary: The entity-ref factory of a registered aggregate.

member.name: T:FCQRS.FcqrsRuntime
member.summary: Runtime registry, populated once at host startup. Holds the live actor system, each aggregate's factory + refs (keyed by the aggregate's CLR type) and the projection subscription. Resolved from DI so endpoints can pull the Handlers / the subscription, and saga registrations can look up the aggregates they wire.

member.name: T:FCQRS.Common.PrefixConversion
member.summary: Default shard name used if no specific sharding strategy is provided. Represents a potential transformation to apply to an entity ID prefix, used in saga routing. Allows sagas to be co-located or routed differently based on the originator's ID structure.

member.name: P:FCQRS.Common.IActor.TimeProvider
member.summary: Gets the time provider.

member.name: P:FCQRS.Common.IActor.System
member.summary: Gets the hosting ActorSystem.

member.name: P:FCQRS.Common.IActor.Mediator
member.summary: Gets the reference to the distributed pub/sub mediator actor.

member.name: P:FCQRS.Common.IActor.Materializer
member.summary: Gets the Akka Streams materializer.

member.name: P:FCQRS.Common.IActor.LoggerFactory
member.summary: Gets the logger factory.

member.name: P:FCQRS.Common.IActor.Configuration
member.summary: Gets the configuration.

member.name: M:FCQRS.Common.IActor.SubscribeForCommand``2(FCQRS.Common.CommandHandler.Command{``0,``1})
member.summary: Subscribes to the result of a command sent to another actor.

member.name: M:FCQRS.Common.IActor.Stop
member.summary: Stops the actor system gracefully.

member.name: M:FCQRS.Common.IActor.InitializeSagaStarter(Microsoft.FSharp.Core.FSharpFunc{System.Object,Microsoft.FSharp.Collections.FSharpList{Microsoft.FSharp.Core.FSharpFunc{System.String,Akkling.Cluster.Sharding.IEntityRef{System.Object}}}})
member.summary: Simplified saga starter where you just return factories. Uses default PrefixConversion (identity) and passes through the original event.

member.name: M:FCQRS.Common.IActor.InitializeSagaStarter(Microsoft.FSharp.Core.FSharpFunc{System.Object,Microsoft.FSharp.Collections.FSharpList{System.Tuple{Microsoft.FSharp.Core.FSharpFunc{System.String,Akkling.Cluster.Sharding.IEntityRef{System.Object}},FCQRS.Common.PrefixConversion,System.Object}}})
member.summary: Initializes the Saga Starter actor, configuring which events trigger which sagas. <param name="eventHandler">A function mapping a received event object to a list of saga definitions to start: `obj -> list<(Factory * Prefix * StartingEvent)>`.</param>

Initializes a sharded, persistent saga actor. <param name="initialState">The initial state (`SagaState`) for new saga instances.</param> <param name="handleEvent">The event handler function: `Event -> SagaState -> EventAction`.</param> <param name="applySideEffects">Function determining side effects based on state transitions: `SagaState -> Option<StartingEvent> -> bool -> SagaTransition<NewState> * ExecuteCommand list`.</param> <param name="applyStateChange">Function to apply internal state changes: `SagaState -> SagaState`.</param> <param name="name">The shard type name for this saga.</param> <returns>An entity factory (`EntityFac<obj>`) for creating instances of this saga.</returns>

Initializes a sharded, persistent aggregate actor. <param name="cfg">Environment configuration (IConfiguration & ILoggerFactory).</param> <param name="initialState">The initial state for new aggregate instances.</param> <param name="name">The shard type name for this aggregate.</param> <param name="handleCommand">The command handler function: `Command -> State -> EventAction`.</param> <param name="apply">The event handler function: `Event -> State -> State`.</param> <returns>An entity factory (`EntityFac<obj>`) for creating instances of this actor.</returns>

Creates a command subscription to wait for a specific event from a target actor. Sends the command and asynchronously returns the first matching event received. <param name="factory">Entity factory function for the target actor type.</param> <param name="cid">Correlation ID for tracking.</param> <param name="id">Entity ID of the target actor.</param> <param name="command">The command payload to send.</param> <param name="filter">A predicate function to select the desired event.</param> <param name="metadata">Optional metadata to include with the command.</param> <returns>An async computation yielding the target event.</returns>

member.name: T:FCQRS.Common.IActor
member.summary: Defines the core functionalities and context provided by the FCQRS environment to actors. This interface provides access to essential Akka.NET services and FCQRS initialization methods.

member.name: P:FCQRS.Common.SagaState`2.State
member.summary: The current state machine state of the saga.

member.name: P:FCQRS.Common.SagaState`2.Data
member.summary: The custom data associated with this saga instance.

member.name: T:FCQRS.Common.SagaState`2
member.summary: Represents the state of a saga instance. <typeparam name="'SagaData">The type of the custom data held by the saga.</typeparam> <typeparam name="'State">The type representing the saga's current state machine state (e.g., an enum or DU).</typeparam>

member.name: T:FCQRS.Common.SagaTransition`1.NextState
member.summary: The saga should transition to a new state

member.name: T:FCQRS.Common.SagaTransition`1.Stay
member.summary: The saga should stay in current state without changes

member.name: T:FCQRS.Common.SagaTransition`1.StopSaga
member.summary: The saga should stop and terminate

member.name: T:FCQRS.Common.SagaTransition`1
member.summary: Represents the next state transition for a saga after processing an event or timeout.

member.name: P:FCQRS.Common.ExecuteCommand.DelayInMs
member.summary: An optional delay in milliseconds before sending the command.

member.name: P:FCQRS.Common.ExecuteCommand.Command
member.summary: The command message to send (boxed).

member.name: P:FCQRS.Common.ExecuteCommand.TargetActor
member.summary: The target actor for the command.

member.name: T:FCQRS.Common.ExecuteCommand
member.summary: Represents a command to be executed, often scheduled or triggered by a saga.

member.name: T:FCQRS.Common.TargetActor.Self
member.summary: Specifies the target as the current saga actor itself.

member.name: T:FCQRS.Common.TargetActor.Sender
member.summary: Specifies the target as the original sender of the message that triggered the current saga step.

member.name: T:FCQRS.Common.TargetActor.ActorRef
member.summary: Specifies the target using its direct IActorRef (usually boxed as obj).

member.name: T:FCQRS.Common.TargetActor.FactoryAndName
member.summary: Specifies the target using a factory function and name.

member.name: T:FCQRS.Common.TargetActor
member.summary: Represents the target of a command execution triggered by a saga.

member.name: P:FCQRS.Common.FactoryAndName.Name
member.summary: The name identifier for the target actor.

member.name: P:FCQRS.Common.FactoryAndName.Factory
member.summary: The factory function (or entity ref creator) used to potentially create the actor.

member.name: T:FCQRS.Common.FactoryAndName
member.summary: Represents the information needed to locate or create a target actor, typically used within sagas.

member.name: T:FCQRS.Common.TargetName.Originator
member.summary: Identify the target as the originator actor of the current saga process.

member.name: T:FCQRS.Common.TargetName.Name
member.summary: Identify the target by its string name (entity ID).

member.name: T:FCQRS.Common.TargetName
member.summary: Represents the name identifying a target actor for a command, typically used within sagas.

member.name: T:FCQRS.Common.SnapshotPolicy.Every
member.summary: Snapshot every N versions (N > 0; invalid values fall back to Default).

member.name: T:FCQRS.Common.SnapshotPolicy.NoSnapshots
member.summary: Never snapshot: recovery always replays the full journal. Previously saved snapshots are still honored on recovery.

member.name: T:FCQRS.Common.SnapshotPolicy.Default
member.summary: Use the global config (config:akka:persistence:snapshot-version-count), or 30.

member.name: T:FCQRS.Common.SnapshotPolicy
member.summary: Snapshot cadence for an aggregate or saga, set per entity at registration.

member.name: T:FCQRS.Common.EventAction`1.StateChangedEvent
member.summary: Indicate that the state of a saga has changed (used internally by sagas for persistence).

member.name: T:FCQRS.Common.EventAction`1.UnhandledEvent
member.summary: Indicate that the command or event could not be handled in the current state.

member.name: T:FCQRS.Common.EventAction`1.IgnoreEvent
member.summary: Ignore the event or command completely. No persistence, publishing, or state update occurs.

member.name: T:FCQRS.Common.EventAction`1.PublishEvent
member.summary: Publish the event immediately to the mediator without persisting it. The actor's state is not updated.

member.name: T:FCQRS.Common.EventAction`1.DeferEvent
member.summary: Defer the event. It will be stashed and processed later, potentially after other events.

member.name: T:FCQRS.Common.EventAction`1.PersistAllEvents
member.summary: Persist several events from one command as a single journal AtomicWrite: all land or none do (one DB transaction in the SQL plugins), and no state update, publish or saga wake-up happens until the whole batch is durable. Versions are allocated sequentially. Atomic for THIS aggregate instance only — atomicity across aggregates is saga territory. Note the read side still projects the batch event-by-event (durability is atomic, read-model visibility is not).

member.name: T:FCQRS.Common.EventAction`1.PersistEvent
member.summary: Persist the event to the journal. The actor's state will be updated using the event handler *after* persistence succeeds.

member.name: T:FCQRS.Common.EventAction`1
member.summary: Defines the possible actions an aggregate or saga actor can take after processing a command or event. <typeparam name="'T">The type of the event payload associated with the action (e.g., for PersistEvent).</typeparam>

member.name: P:FCQRS.Common.Event`1.Metadata
member.summary: Metadata associated with the event.

member.name: P:FCQRS.Common.Event`1.Version
member.summary: The version number of the aggregate after this event was applied.

member.name: P:FCQRS.Common.Event`1.CorrelationId
member.summary: The correlation ID linking the event back to the originating command.

member.name: P:FCQRS.Common.Event`1.Sender
member.summary: An optional identifier for the actor that generated the event.

member.name: P:FCQRS.Common.Event`1.Id
member.summary: A unique identifier for the message.

member.name: P:FCQRS.Common.Event`1.CreationDate
member.summary: The timestamp when the event was created.

member.name: P:FCQRS.Common.Event`1.EventDetails
member.summary: The specific details or payload of the event.

member.name: T:FCQRS.Common.Event`1
member.summary: Represents an event generated by an aggregate actor as a result of processing a command. <typeparam name="'EventDetails">The specific type of the event payload.</typeparam>

member.name: P:FCQRS.Common.Command`1.Metadata
member.summary: Metadata associated with the command.

member.name: P:FCQRS.Common.Command`1.CorrelationId
member.summary: The correlation ID used to track the command through the system.

member.name: P:FCQRS.Common.Command`1.Sender
member.summary: An optional identifier for the actor that sent the command.

member.name: P:FCQRS.Common.Command`1.Id
member.summary: A unique identifier for the message.

member.name: P:FCQRS.Common.Command`1.CreationDate
member.summary: The timestamp when the command was created.

member.name: P:FCQRS.Common.Command`1.CommandDetails
member.summary: The specific details or payload of the command.

member.name: T:FCQRS.Common.Command`1
member.summary: Represents a command to be processed by an aggregate actor. <typeparam name="'CommandDetails">The specific type of the command payload.</typeparam>

member.name: P:FCQRS.Common.IEnvelope.Payload
member.summary: The command/event details, boxed.

member.name: T:FCQRS.Common.IEnvelope
member.summary: Non-generic access to a Command<_>/Event<_> payload, so a boxed message can be unwrapped to its details without knowing the concrete payload type.

member.name: T:FCQRS.Common.ISerializable
member.summary: Marker interface for types that can be serialized by Akka.NET.

member.name: M:FCQRS.Common.sameTrace(FCQRS.Model.Data.CID,FCQRS.Model.Data.CID)
member.summary: Compare two CIDs by their TraceId (for correlation in distributed tracing scenarios)

member.name: M:FCQRS.Common.extractTraceId(FCQRS.Model.Data.CID)
member.summary: Helper to extract TraceId from a CID (W3C traceparent format: 00-{traceId}-{spanId}-{flags}) Returns the full CID string if not in traceparent format

member.name: T:FCQRS.Common.CommandHandler.Command`2
member.summary: Represents the message sent to the internal subscription mechanism. <typeparam name="'Command">The type of the command payload.</typeparam> <typeparam name="'Event">The type of the expected event payload.</typeparam>

member.name: T:FCQRS.Common.CommandHandler
member.summary: (Internal) Contains the implementation for command subscriptions.

member.name: P:FCQRS.Common.SagaStarter.SagaStartingEvent`1.Event
member.summary: The actual event payload that triggers the saga.

member.name: T:FCQRS.Common.SagaStarter.SagaStartingEvent`1
member.summary: Wraps an event that is intended to start a saga. This is typically the message sent to a saga actor upon its creation. <typeparam name="'T">The type of the starting event payload.</typeparam>

member.name: T:FCQRS.Common.SagaStarter
member.summary: Contains types and functions related to the Saga Starter actor (internal implementation detail).

member.name: T:FCQRS.Common.SagaBuilder.SagaStateWrapper`2
member.summary: Standard wrapper for saga states that includes NotStarted/Started
Simplified saga initialization with unwrapped apply function
High-level saga initialization that handles all wrapping automatically

member.name: M:FCQRS.Common.SagaBuilder.wrapApply``3(Microsoft.FSharp.Core.FSharpFunc{FCQRS.Common.SagaState{``0,``1},FCQRS.Common.SagaState{``0,``1}},FCQRS.Common.SagaState{``0,FCQRS.Common.SagaBuilder.SagaStateWrapper{``1,``2}})
member.summary: Wraps user's apply function to handle NotStarted/Started automatically
Wraps user's handleEvent to skip NotStarted but allow Started states
Wraps user's applySideEffects to handle NotStarted/Started automatically

member.name: M:FCQRS.Common.SagaBuilder.createInitialState``3(``0)
member.summary: Creates initial saga state with NotStarted

member.name: M:FCQRS.Common.SagaBuilder.handleStartedState``2(System.Boolean,Microsoft.FSharp.Core.FSharpOption{FCQRS.Common.SagaStarter.SagaStartingEvent{FCQRS.Common.Event{``0}}},Microsoft.FSharp.Core.FSharpFunc{System.String,Akkling.Cluster.Sharding.IEntityRef{System.Object}})
member.summary: Standard recovery logic for Started state that all sagas should use Handles the version checking handshake with the originator aggregate

member.name: T:FCQRS.Common.SagaBuilder
member.summary: Contains types and functions for building and initializing sagas

member.name: T:FCQRS.Common
member.summary: Contains common types like Events and Commands

member.name: M:FCQRS.DynamicConfig.ConfigExtension.GetSectionAsDynamic(Microsoft.Extensions.Configuration.IConfiguration,System.String)
member.summary: An extension method that returns given string as an dynamic Expando object
member.exception: Thrown configuration or section is null

member.name: M:FCQRS.DynamicConfig.ConfigExtension.GetRootAsDynamic(Microsoft.Extensions.Configuration.IConfiguration)
member.summary: An extension method that returns given string as an dynamic Expando object
member.returns: An expando object represents given section

member.name: M:FCQRS.Scheduler.ObservingScheduler.remove_OnEnqueued(Microsoft.FSharp.Control.FSharpHandler{System.Tuple{Microsoft.FSharp.Core.FSharpOption{System.String},System.TimeSpan,Microsoft.FSharp.Core.FSharpFunc{Microsoft.FSharp.Core.Unit,Microsoft.FSharp.Core.Unit}}})
member.summary: Fired whenever a ScheduledItem is enqueued

member.name: M:FCQRS.Scheduler.ObservingScheduler.remove_OnCancelled(Microsoft.FSharp.Control.FSharpHandler{System.Tuple{Microsoft.FSharp.Core.FSharpOption{System.String},System.TimeSpan,Microsoft.FSharp.Core.FSharpFunc{Microsoft.FSharp.Core.Unit,Microsoft.FSharp.Core.Unit}}})
member.summary: Fired whenever a ScheduledItem is cancelled

member.name: P:FCQRS.Scheduler.ObservingScheduler.OnEnqueued
member.summary: Fired whenever a ScheduledItem is enqueued

member.name: P:FCQRS.Scheduler.ObservingScheduler.OnCancelled
member.summary: Fired whenever a ScheduledItem is cancelled

member.name: M:FCQRS.Scheduler.ObservingScheduler.add_OnEnqueued(Microsoft.FSharp.Control.FSharpHandler{System.Tuple{Microsoft.FSharp.Core.FSharpOption{System.String},System.TimeSpan,Microsoft.FSharp.Core.FSharpFunc{Microsoft.FSharp.Core.Unit,Microsoft.FSharp.Core.Unit}}})
member.summary: Fired whenever a ScheduledItem is enqueued

member.name: M:FCQRS.Scheduler.ObservingScheduler.add_OnCancelled(Microsoft.FSharp.Control.FSharpHandler{System.Tuple{Microsoft.FSharp.Core.FSharpOption{System.String},System.TimeSpan,Microsoft.FSharp.Core.FSharpFunc{Microsoft.FSharp.Core.Unit,Microsoft.FSharp.Core.Unit}}})
member.summary: Fired whenever a ScheduledItem is cancelled

member.summary: Fallback ScheduleTellRepeatedly without name

member.name: M:FCQRS.Scheduler.ObservingScheduler.ScheduleTellRepeatedly(Microsoft.FSharp.Core.FSharpOption{System.String},System.TimeSpan,System.TimeSpan,Akka.Actor.ICanTell,System.Object,Akka.Actor.IActorRef)
member.summary: Schedule repeated Tell with optional name

member.name: M:FCQRS.Scheduler.ObservingScheduler.ScheduleTellOnce(System.TimeSpan,Akka.Actor.ICanTell,System.Object,Akka.Actor.IActorRef)
member.summary: Fallback ScheduleTellOnce without name

member.name: M:FCQRS.Scheduler.ObservingScheduler.ScheduleTellOnce(Microsoft.FSharp.Core.FSharpOption{System.String},System.TimeSpan,Akka.Actor.ICanTell,System.Object,Akka.Actor.IActorRef)
member.summary: Schedule a one-time Tell with optional name

member.name: M:FCQRS.Scheduler.ObservingScheduler.ScheduleRepeatedly(System.TimeSpan,System.TimeSpan,Microsoft.FSharp.Core.FSharpFunc{Microsoft.FSharp.Core.Unit,Microsoft.FSharp.Core.Unit})
member.summary: Fallback ScheduleRepeatedly without name

member.name: M:FCQRS.Scheduler.ObservingScheduler.ScheduleRepeatedly(Microsoft.FSharp.Core.FSharpOption{System.String},System.TimeSpan,System.TimeSpan,Microsoft.FSharp.Core.FSharpFunc{Microsoft.FSharp.Core.Unit,Microsoft.FSharp.Core.Unit})
member.summary: Schedule a repeated action with optional name

member.name: M:FCQRS.Scheduler.ObservingScheduler.ScheduleOnce(System.TimeSpan,Microsoft.FSharp.Core.FSharpFunc{Microsoft.FSharp.Core.Unit,Microsoft.FSharp.Core.Unit})
member.summary: Fallback ScheduleOnce without name

member.name: M:FCQRS.Scheduler.ObservingScheduler.ScheduleOnce(Microsoft.FSharp.Core.FSharpOption{System.String},System.TimeSpan,Microsoft.FSharp.Core.FSharpFunc{Microsoft.FSharp.Core.Unit,Microsoft.FSharp.Core.Unit})
member.summary: Schedule a one-time action with optional name

member.name: M:FCQRS.Scheduler.ObservingScheduler.Cancel(Akka.Actor.ICancelable,Microsoft.FSharp.Core.FSharpOption{System.String})
member.summary: Cancel via wrapper with name

member.name: M:FCQRS.Scheduler.ObservingScheduler.Advance(System.TimeSpan)
member.summary: Drive the virtual clock forward

member.name: T:FCQRS.Scheduler.ObservingScheduler
member.summary: Scheduler wrapper that exposes enqueue/cancel events with optional names

member.name: T:FCQRS.Scheduler.NamedCancelable
member.summary: Proxy that carries a name through cancellation - uses a CancellationTokenSource to handle cancellation

member.name: M:FCQRS.SchedulerController.stop
member.summary: (Optional) Stops the scheduler controller agent.

member.name: M:FCQRS.SchedulerController.registerAutoAdvanceOnAppearance(System.String)
member.summary: Registers a task for automatic advancement upon its appearance.

member.name: M:FCQRS.SchedulerController.signalAndAdvanceForCapturedTask
member.summary: Signals the controller to advance for a previously captured task and resume normal advancement.

member.name: M:FCQRS.SchedulerController.watchForAndPauseOnNext(System.String)
member.summary: Instructs the controller to watch for the next occurrence of a specific task name.

member.name: M:FCQRS.SchedulerController.start(Akka.Actor.IScheduler)
member.summary: Initializes and starts the SchedulerController agent. This should be called once during application startup.

member.name: T:FCQRS.Actor.Connection
member.summary: Represents a database connection configuration

member.name: T:FCQRS.Actor.DBType.DB2
member.summary: IBM DB2

member.name: T:FCQRS.Actor.DBType.Firebird
member.summary: Firebird

member.name: T:FCQRS.Actor.DBType.Oracle
member.summary: Oracle Database

member.name: T:FCQRS.Actor.DBType.MySql
member.summary: MySQL using MySqlConnector

member.name: T:FCQRS.Actor.DBType.PostgreSQL15
member.summary: PostgreSQL 15+

member.name: T:FCQRS.Actor.DBType.PostgreSQL
member.summary: PostgreSQL 9.3+

member.name: T:FCQRS.Actor.DBType.SqlServer2022
member.summary: Microsoft SQL Server 2022

member.name: T:FCQRS.Actor.DBType.SqlServer2019
member.summary: Microsoft SQL Server 2019

member.name: T:FCQRS.Actor.DBType.SqlServer2017
member.summary: Microsoft SQL Server 2017

member.name: T:FCQRS.Actor.DBType.SqlServer2016
member.summary: Microsoft SQL Server 2016

member.name: T:FCQRS.Actor.DBType.SqlServer2014
member.summary: Microsoft SQL Server 2014

member.name: T:FCQRS.Actor.DBType.SqlServer2012
member.summary: Microsoft SQL Server 2012

member.name: T:FCQRS.Actor.DBType.Sqlite
member.summary: SQLite using Microsoft.Data.Sqlite provider

member.name: T:FCQRS.Actor.DBType
member.summary: Represents the type of database connection

member.name: T:FCQRS.Actor.HoconStringConfigurationSource
member.summary: Custom configuration source for in-memory HOCON strings

member.name: T:FCQRS.Actor.HoconStringConfigurationProvider
member.summary: Custom configuration provider for in-memory HOCON strings

member.name: T:FCQRS.Query.ISubscribe
member.summary: The canonical subscription stream: a non-generic shorthand for ISubscribe<IMessageWithCID> — the type every FCQRS projection / read-your-writes subscription actually uses (cf. IEnumerable vs IEnumerable<T>). Lets consumers write ISubscribe instead of the closed generic, and inject it by that name.

member.name: M:FCQRS.Query.ISubscribe`1.Subscribe(FCQRS.Model.Data.CID,Microsoft.FSharp.Core.FSharpFunc{`0,System.Boolean},System.Int32,Microsoft.FSharp.Core.FSharpOption{Microsoft.FSharp.Core.FSharpFunc{`0,Microsoft.FSharp.Core.Unit}},Microsoft.FSharp.Core.FSharpOption{System.Threading.CancellationToken})
member.summary: Subscribes to events matching a specific correlation ID and an additional filter.
member.param: Additional predicate to filter events after CID matching.
Maximum number of events to process.
Optional callback function to handle the event.
An optional cancellation token to cancel the subscription.

member.name: M:FCQRS.Query.ISubscribe`1.Subscribe(FCQRS.Model.Data.CID,System.Int32,Microsoft.FSharp.Core.FSharpOption{Microsoft.FSharp.Core.FSharpFunc{`0,Microsoft.FSharp.Core.Unit}},Microsoft.FSharp.Core.FSharpOption{System.Threading.CancellationToken})
member.summary: Subscribes to events matching a specific correlation ID.
member.param: Maximum number of events to process.
Optional callback function to handle the event.
An optional cancellation token to cancel the subscription.

member.name: M:FCQRS.Query.ISubscribe`1.Subscribe(Microsoft.FSharp.Core.FSharpFunc{`0,System.Boolean},System.Int32,Microsoft.FSharp.Core.FSharpOption{Microsoft.FSharp.Core.FSharpFunc{`0,Microsoft.FSharp.Core.Unit}},Microsoft.FSharp.Core.FSharpOption{System.Threading.CancellationToken})
member.summary: Subscribes to events using a filter. Only events for which the predicate returns true are processed, and the callback is invoked for each matching event up to a specified count.
member.param: Predicate function to determine if an event should be processed, e.g. fun event -> event.CorrelationId = targetId.
Maximum number of events to process.
Optional callback function to handle the event (defaults to ignoring the event if not provided).
An optional cancellation token to cancel the subscription.

member.name: M:FCQRS.Query.ISubscribe`1.Subscribe(Microsoft.FSharp.Core.FSharpFunc{`0,Microsoft.FSharp.Core.Unit},Microsoft.FSharp.Core.FSharpOption{System.Threading.CancellationToken})
member.summary: Subscribes to all events and invokes the specified callback for each event.
member.param: Function invoked for each event, e.g. printing or processing the event.
An optional cancellation token to cancel the subscription.

member.name: M:FCQRS.Query.asDefaultSubscribe(FCQRS.Query.ISubscribe{FCQRS.Model.Data.IMessageWithCID})
member.summary: Adapt a generic ISubscribe<IMessageWithCID> to the non-generic ISubscribe (forwards every Subscribe overload to the inner subscription).

member.name: P:FCQRS.FSharp.Projection.Handle
member.summary: Called per persisted event; returns the read-model events to publish to subscribers (empty list = nothing to notify).

member.name: P:FCQRS.FSharp.Projection.LastOffset
member.summary: Resume from this journal offset (e.g. the last committed offset).

member.name: T:FCQRS.FSharp.Projection
member.summary: A read-model projection definition.

member.name: T:FCQRS.FSharp.SagaHandle
member.summary: What you get back after registering a saga.

member.name: P:FCQRS.FSharp.Saga`3.Snapshots
member.summary: Snapshot cadence: Default (config / 30), NoSnapshots, or Every n.

member.name: P:FCQRS.FSharp.Saga`3.StartOn
member.summary: Which originator events spawn an instance of this saga. Typed to the originator's event so 'OriginatorEvent is inferred from the definition — there is no type argument to remember (or to get silently wrong).

member.name: P:FCQRS.FSharp.Saga`3.Originator
member.summary: The aggregate the saga starts from (its commands' Originator target).

member.name: T:FCQRS.FSharp.Saga`3
member.summary: A pure saga definition. HandleEvent is obj-based so a single saga can match events from several aggregates; ApplySideEffects returns the transition plus the commands to dispatch.

member.name: P:FCQRS.FSharp.AggregateHandle`2.Send
member.summary: Send a command and await the first matching event (read-your-writes).

member.name: P:FCQRS.FSharp.AggregateHandle`2.Factory
member.summary: Entity-ref factory (DEFAULT_SHARD applied) — hand this to a saga to target it.

member.name: T:FCQRS.FSharp.AggregateHandle`2
member.summary: What you get back after registering an aggregate.

member.name: P:FCQRS.FSharp.Aggregate`3.Snapshots
member.summary: Snapshot cadence: Default (config / 30), NoSnapshots, or Every n.

member.name: P:FCQRS.FSharp.Aggregate`3.Fold
member.summary: applyEvent (fold): event + current state -> next state (pure).

member.name: P:FCQRS.FSharp.Aggregate`3.Decide
member.summary: handleCommand (decide): command + current state -> what to do.

member.name: T:FCQRS.FSharp.Aggregate`3
member.summary: A pure aggregate definition: the four things that define an event-sourced aggregate. No wiring, no IActor.

member.name: T:FCQRS.FSharp.AggregateFactory
member.summary: An aggregate's entity-ref factory: an id -> its sharded actor ref.

member.name: M:FCQRS.FSharp.toOriginatorAfter(Microsoft.FSharp.Core.FSharpFunc{System.String,Akkling.Cluster.Sharding.IEntityRef{System.Object}},System.Int64,System.String,System.Object)
member.summary: Delayed variant of toOriginator (delayMs, taskName key).

member.name: M:FCQRS.FSharp.toActor(Akkling.ActorRefs.IActorRef{System.Object},System.Object)
member.summary: Send a command to a concrete actor ref.

member.name: M:FCQRS.FSharp.toAggregate(Microsoft.FSharp.Core.FSharpFunc{System.String,Akkling.Cluster.Sharding.IEntityRef{System.Object}},System.String,System.Object)
member.summary: Send a command to a specific aggregate instance by id (cross-aggregate).

member.name: M:FCQRS.FSharp.toOriginator(Microsoft.FSharp.Core.FSharpFunc{System.String,Akkling.Cluster.Sharding.IEntityRef{System.Object}},System.Object)
member.summary: Send a command back to the saga's originator aggregate.

member.name: M:FCQRS.FSharp.Fcqrs.projection(FCQRS.Common.IActor,FCQRS.FSharp.Projection)
member.summary: Register the read-model projection and return the subscription stream.

member.name: M:FCQRS.FSharp.Fcqrs.wireSagaStarters(FCQRS.Common.IActor,Microsoft.FSharp.Collections.FSharpList{FCQRS.FSharp.SagaHandle})
member.summary: Wire every registered saga into one saga-starter (or the empty starter if none). Call after the aggregates + sagas are registered.

member.name: M:FCQRS.FSharp.Fcqrs.saga``3(FCQRS.Common.IActor,FCQRS.FSharp.Saga{``0,``1,``2})
M:FCQRS.FSharp.Fcqrs.aggregate``3(FCQRS.Common.IActor,FCQRS.FSharp.Aggregate{``0,``1,``2})
member.summary: Register an aggregate and return its typed handle. Calling this IS the registration (it initializes the sharding region).

member.name: M:FCQRS.FSharp.Fcqrs.aggregateId(System.String)
member.summary: An aggregate id from a string (e.g. a document/user key).

member.name: M:FCQRS.FSharp.Fcqrs.newCid
member.summary: A fresh correlation id (UUID v7).

member.name: M:FCQRS.FSharp.Fcqrs.actor(Microsoft.Extensions.Configuration.IConfiguration,Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.ILoggerFactory,Microsoft.FSharp.Core.FSharpOption{FCQRS.Actor.Connection},System.String)
member.summary: Create the actor system from plain values (cluster name as a string).

member.name: M:FCQRS.FSharp.Fcqrs.connect(FCQRS.Actor.DBType,System.String)
member.summary: Build a SQLite/etc. Connection from a raw connection string (ShortString hidden).

member.name: M:FCQRS.FSharp.Fcqrs.refFor(Akkling.Cluster.Sharding.EntityFac{System.Object},System.String)
member.summary: The single place DEFAULT_SHARD / RefFor is applied (mirrors CSharpInterop).

member.name: T:FCQRS.FSharp
P:FCQRS.CSharp.Saga`3.SnapshotPolicy
member.summary: Per-saga snapshot cadence. Override to use Every(n) or NoSnapshots; the default falls back to config:akka:persistence:snapshot-version-count (or 30).

member.name: M:FCQRS.CSharp.Saga`3.StateChanged(`2)
member.summary: Transition / event DSL — over TState.

member.name: M:FCQRS.CSharp.Saga`3.Init(FCQRS.Common.IActor)
member.summary: Register the saga; calling this IS the registration.

member.name: M:FCQRS.CSharp.Saga`3.Factory(FCQRS.Common.IActor)
member.summary: The factory the saga-starter spawns instances from.

member.name: T:FCQRS.CSharp.Saga`3
member.summary: C#-friendly abstract base for a saga. A concrete saga supplies InitialData, SagaName, Originator (the aggregate it starts from), HandleEvent and ApplySideEffects; the base provides the wiring (Init/Factory) and the small transition DSL. Designed to be subclassed from C#.

member.name: P:FCQRS.CSharp.Aggregate`3.SnapshotPolicy
member.summary: Per-aggregate snapshot cadence. Override to use Every(n) or NoSnapshots; the default falls back to config:akka:persistence:snapshot-version-count (or 30).

member.name: M:FCQRS.CSharp.Aggregate`3.Init(FCQRS.Common.IActor)
member.summary: Register the aggregate and hand back its Factory + Handler.

member.name: T:FCQRS.CSharp.Aggregate`3
member.summary: C#-friendly abstract base for an event-sourced aggregate. A concrete aggregate supplies only InitialState, EntityName, HandleCommand (the decision) and ApplyEvent (the fold); the base provides the wiring (Init). Designed to be subclassed from C#.

member.name: M:FCQRS.CSharp.SagaApi.InitSimple``3(FCQRS.Common.IActor,``1,System.Func{FCQRS.Common.Event{``0},``1,``2,FCQRS.Common.EventAction{``2}},System.Func{``1,``2,System.Boolean,FCQRS.CSharp.SagaSideEffectResult{``2}},FCQRS.CSharp.AggregateFactory,System.String)
member.summary: Initialize a saga with simplified signatures and no apply

member.name: M:FCQRS.CSharp.SagaApi.InitSimple``3(FCQRS.Common.IActor,``1,System.Func{FCQRS.Common.Event{``0},``1,``2,FCQRS.Common.EventAction{``2}},System.Func{``1,``2,System.Boolean,FCQRS.CSharp.SagaSideEffectResult{``2}},System.Func{``1,``2,``1},FCQRS.CSharp.AggregateFactory,System.String)
member.summary: Initialize a saga with simplified signatures - no FSharpOption, typed events handleEvent: (event, data, currentState) -> EventAction applySideEffects: (data, state, recovering) -> SagaSideEffectResult
Initialize a saga without apply (no data updates based on state)
Initialize a saga with simplified apply - just takes data and unwrapped state, returns new data
Initialize a saga with an explicit per-saga snapshot policy.
Initialize a saga with C# delegates 'TEvent - The triggering event type (from the originator aggregate) 'TSagaData - Cross-cutting saga data 'TSagaState - User-defined saga states

member.name: M:FCQRS.CSharp.SagaApi.Factory(FCQRS.Common.IActor,Akkling.Cluster.Sharding.EntityFac{System.Object},System.String)
member.summary: Get the factory for a saga

member.name: T:FCQRS.CSharp.SagaApi
member.summary: Low-level C# saga API (mirrors ActorApi/QueryApi). For most sagas, prefer the Saga<_,_,_> base class, which wraps this.

member.name: M:FCQRS.CSharp.SagaCommands.ToOriginatorDelayed(FCQRS.CSharp.AggregateFactory,System.Object,System.Int64,System.String)
member.summary: Create a delayed command

member.name: M:FCQRS.CSharp.SagaCommands.ToOriginator(FCQRS.CSharp.AggregateFactory,System.Object)
member.summary: Create a command to send to the originator aggregate

member.name: M:FCQRS.CSharp.SagaCommands.ToAggregate(FCQRS.CSharp.AggregateFactory,System.String,System.Object)
member.summary: Create a command to send to a named aggregate

member.name: M:FCQRS.CSharp.SagaCommands.ToActor(Akkling.ActorRefs.IActorRef{System.Object},System.Object)
member.summary: Create a command to send to an actor ref

member.name: T:FCQRS.CSharp.SagaCommands
member.summary: C#-friendly saga command targeting

member.name: M:FCQRS.CSharp.SagaEventActions.Unhandled``1
member.summary: Event was not handled

member.name: M:FCQRS.CSharp.SagaEventActions.StateChanged``1(``0)
member.summary: Create a state change event

member.name: M:FCQRS.CSharp.SagaEventActions.Ignore``1
member.summary: Ignore the event

member.name: T:FCQRS.CSharp.SagaEventActions
member.summary: C#-friendly factory methods for saga EventAction

member.name: M:FCQRS.CSharp.SagaTransitions.StopSaga``1
member.summary: Stop the saga (completes the saga lifecycle)

member.name: M:FCQRS.CSharp.SagaTransitions.Stay``1
member.summary: Stay in the current state (no transition)

member.name: M:FCQRS.CSharp.SagaTransitions.NextState``1(``0)
member.summary: Transition to a new state

member.name: T:FCQRS.CSharp.SagaTransitions
member.summary: C#-friendly factory methods for SagaTransition

member.name: M:FCQRS.CSharp.SagaStates.WithState``2(FCQRS.Common.SagaState{``0,``1},``1)
member.summary: Create a new SagaState with updated State

member.name: M:FCQRS.CSharp.SagaStates.WithData``2(FCQRS.Common.SagaState{``0,``1},``0)
member.summary: Create a new SagaState with updated Data

member.name: T:FCQRS.CSharp.SagaStates
member.summary: C#-friendly helpers for SagaState

member.name: P:FCQRS.CSharp.SagaSideEffectResult`1.CommandsList
member.summary: Convert commands to F# list for internal use

member.name: T:FCQRS.CSharp.SagaSideEffectResult`1
member.summary: C#-friendly result type for saga side effects (uses class for C# object initializer syntax)

member.name: M:FCQRS.CSharp.ISubscribeExtensions.SubscribeFor``1(FCQRS.Query.ISubscribe{``0},FCQRS.Model.Data.CID,System.Func{``0,System.Boolean},System.Int32)
member.summary: C#-friendly Subscribe that matches by CID and additional filter

member.name: M:FCQRS.CSharp.ISubscribeExtensions.SubscribeFor``1(FCQRS.Query.ISubscribe{``0},FCQRS.Model.Data.CID,System.Int32)
member.summary: C#-friendly Subscribe that matches by CID only

member.name: M:FCQRS.CSharp.ISubscribeExtensions.SubscribeFor``1(FCQRS.Query.ISubscribe{``0},System.Func{``0,System.Boolean},System.Int32)
member.summary: C#-friendly Subscribe that accepts Func instead of FSharpFunc

member.name: T:FCQRS.CSharp.ISubscribeExtensions
member.summary: Extension methods for ISubscribe

member.name: T:FCQRS.CSharp.IActorExtensions
member.summary: Obsolete alias for <see cref="T:FCQRS.CSharp.ActorWiring"/>. Renamed because most members are static wiring helpers, not extension methods.

member.name: M:FCQRS.CSharp.ActorWiring.SendCommandAsync``2(FCQRS.Common.IActor,FCQRS.CSharp.AggregateFactory,FCQRS.Model.Data.CID,FCQRS.Model.Data.AggregateId,``1,System.Func{``0,System.Boolean})
member.summary: Send a command and wait for the event (C# friendly)

member.name: M:FCQRS.CSharp.ActorWiring.InitializeSagaStarterEmpty(FCQRS.Common.IActor)
member.summary: Initialize saga starter with no sagas (for simple scenarios)

member.name: M:FCQRS.CSharp.ActorWiring.InitSagaStarterSimple(FCQRS.Common.IActor,System.Func{System.Object,System.Collections.Generic.IList{FCQRS.CSharp.AggregateFactory}})
member.summary: C#-friendly simplified InitializeSagaStarter where you just return factories

member.name: M:FCQRS.CSharp.ActorWiring.InitSagaStarterEmpty(FCQRS.Common.IActor)
member.summary: Static helper for C# where extension may not resolve

member.name: M:FCQRS.CSharp.ActorWiring.InitSagaStarter(FCQRS.Common.IActor,System.Func{System.Object,System.Collections.Generic.IList{FCQRS.CSharp.SagaDefinition}})
member.summary: C#-friendly InitializeSagaStarter that accepts Func returning IList of SagaDefinition

member.name: M:FCQRS.CSharp.ActorWiring.InitAggregate``3(FCQRS.Common.IActor,``0,System.String,System.Func{FCQRS.Common.Command{``1},``0,FCQRS.Common.EventAction{``2}},System.Func{FCQRS.Common.Event{``2},``0,``0},FCQRS.Common.SnapshotPolicy)
member.summary: InitAggregate with an explicit per-aggregate snapshot policy.

member.name: M:FCQRS.CSharp.ActorWiring.InitAggregate``3(FCQRS.Common.IActor,``0,System.String,System.Func{FCQRS.Common.Command{``1},``0,FCQRS.Common.EventAction{``2}},System.Func{FCQRS.Common.Event{``2},``0,``0})
member.summary: One-call aggregate wiring: registers the aggregate (sharding region) and returns its Factory + Handler. Collapses the Init/Factory/Handler trio an aggregate would otherwise hand-roll. Call it for EVERY aggregate you want live — registration is the act of calling this, not a side effect.

member.name: M:FCQRS.CSharp.ActorWiring.InitActor``3(FCQRS.Common.IActor,``0,System.String,System.Func{FCQRS.Common.Command{``1},``0,FCQRS.Common.EventAction{``2}},System.Func{FCQRS.Common.Event{``2},``0,``0},FCQRS.Common.SnapshotPolicy)
member.summary: InitActor with an explicit per-aggregate snapshot policy.

member.name: M:FCQRS.CSharp.ActorWiring.InitActor``3(FCQRS.Common.IActor,``0,System.String,System.Func{FCQRS.Common.Command{``1},``0,FCQRS.Common.EventAction{``2}},System.Func{FCQRS.Common.Event{``2},``0,``0})
member.summary: C#-friendly InitializeActor that accepts Func delegates instead of F# functions

member.name: M:FCQRS.CSharp.ActorWiring.CreateCommand``2(FCQRS.Common.IActor,FCQRS.CSharp.AggregateFactory,FCQRS.Model.Data.CID,FCQRS.Model.Data.AggregateId,``1,System.Func{``0,System.Boolean})
member.summary: C#-friendly CreateCommandSubscription that returns FSharpAsync (for use with Handler delegate)

member.name: T:FCQRS.CSharp.ActorWiring
member.summary: Low-level wiring over an IActor: register the saga-starter, aggregates and actors, and send commands. Most members are plain static helpers (you call ActorWiring.Foo(actor, …)); a couple are genuine extension methods. For app composition, prefer the host-builder API (AddFcqrs/AddAggregate/AddSaga).

member.name: M:FCQRS.CSharp.QueryApi.InitWithList(FCQRS.Common.IActor,System.Int32,System.Func{System.Int64,System.Object,System.Collections.Generic.IList{FCQRS.Model.Data.IMessageWithCID}})
member.summary: Obsolete alias — InitWithList is now just an overload of Init.

member.name: M:FCQRS.CSharp.QueryApi.Init(FCQRS.Common.IActor,System.Int32,System.Func{System.Int64,System.Object,System.Collections.Generic.IList{FCQRS.Model.Data.IMessageWithCID}})
member.summary: Initialize the query subscription (C# IList version - converts to F# list internally). An overload of Init, distinguished by the handler's return type.

member.name: M:FCQRS.CSharp.QueryApi.Init(FCQRS.Common.IActor,System.Int32,System.Func{System.Int64,System.Object,Microsoft.FSharp.Collections.FSharpList{FCQRS.Model.Data.IMessageWithCID}})
member.summary: Initialize the query subscription (F# list version)

member.name: T:FCQRS.CSharp.QueryApi
member.summary: C#-friendly Query API

member.name: M:FCQRS.CSharp.ActorApi.Create(Microsoft.Extensions.Configuration.IConfiguration,Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.ILoggerFactory,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Create the actor system with SQLite connection

member.name: T:FCQRS.CSharp.ActorApi
member.summary: C#-friendly Actor API

member.name: P:FCQRS.CSharp.PrefixConversions.Identity
member.summary: Identity conversion - uses originator prefix with saga suffix This creates saga IDs like: originatorId~Saga~correlationId

member.name: M:FCQRS.CSharp.PrefixConversions.Custom(System.Func{System.String,System.String})
member.summary: Custom conversion function

member.name: T:FCQRS.CSharp.PrefixConversions
member.summary: C#-friendly factory for PrefixConversion

member.name: F:FCQRS.CSharp.SagaDefinition.StartingEvent@
member.summary: The event to send to start the saga

member.name: F:FCQRS.CSharp.SagaDefinition.PrefixConversion@
member.summary: How to derive saga entity ID from source entity ID. Defaults to identity (originatorId~Saga~correlationId). The previous default of `PrefixConversion None` produced saga names without the ~Saga~ marker, which breaks originator/saga name resolution and the SagaStarter Continue→SagaCheckDone handshake.

member.name: F:FCQRS.CSharp.SagaDefinition.Factory@
member.summary: Factory function to create entity reference from entity ID

member.name: P:FCQRS.CSharp.SagaDefinition.StartingEvent(System.Object)
member.summary: The event to send to start the saga

member.name: P:FCQRS.CSharp.SagaDefinition.PrefixConversion(FCQRS.Common.PrefixConversion)
member.summary: How to derive saga entity ID from source entity ID. Defaults to identity (originatorId~Saga~correlationId). The previous default of `PrefixConversion None` produced saga names without the ~Saga~ marker, which breaks originator/saga name resolution and the SagaStarter Continue→SagaCheckDone handshake.

member.name: P:FCQRS.CSharp.SagaDefinition.Factory(FCQRS.CSharp.AggregateFactory)
member.summary: Factory function to create entity reference from entity ID

member.name: P:FCQRS.CSharp.SagaDefinition.StartingEvent
member.summary: The event to send to start the saga

member.name: P:FCQRS.CSharp.SagaDefinition.PrefixConversion
member.summary: How to derive saga entity ID from source entity ID. Defaults to identity (originatorId~Saga~correlationId). The previous default of `PrefixConversion None` produced saga names without the ~Saga~ marker, which breaks originator/saga name resolution and the SagaStarter Continue→SagaCheckDone handshake.

member.name: P:FCQRS.CSharp.SagaDefinition.Factory
member.summary: Factory function to create entity reference from entity ID

member.name: T:FCQRS.CSharp.SagaDefinition
member.summary: C#-friendly class for defining saga starters (uses class for C# object initializer syntax)

member.name: M:FCQRS.CSharp.TestEnvelope.Event``1(``0,System.Int64)
member.summary: Wrap an event payload in an Event envelope using the system clock.

member.name: M:FCQRS.CSharp.TestEnvelope.Event``1(``0,System.Int64,System.TimeProvider)
member.summary: Wrap an event payload in an Event envelope carrying the given version, stamping CreationDate from the given TimeProvider.

member.name: M:FCQRS.CSharp.TestEnvelope.Command``1(``0)
member.summary: Wrap a command payload in a Command envelope using the system clock.

member.name: M:FCQRS.CSharp.TestEnvelope.Command``1(``0,System.TimeProvider)
member.summary: Wrap a command payload in a Command envelope, stamping CreationDate from the given TimeProvider. Pass a FakeTimeProvider to test time-dependent decision logic (e.g. sliding-window quotas) deterministically.

member.name: T:FCQRS.CSharp.TestEnvelope
member.summary: C#-friendly builders for the Command/Event envelopes that the pure handleCommand/applyEvent functions expect. Intended for unit tests: the envelope's plumbing fields (a fresh MessageId/CID, a UTC timestamp, no sender, empty metadata) are filled in for you, so a test supplies only the payload — and, for events, the aggregate version. The framework builds these envelopes itself at runtime; tests are the one place you build them by hand.

member.name: M:FCQRS.CSharp.Describe.Case(System.Object)
member.summary: A short case name, e.g. "CreateOrUpdate" for a DocumentCommand.CreateOrUpdate (or a Command/Event wrapping one). Returns "<none>" for null.

member.name: T:FCQRS.CSharp.Describe
member.summary: Diagnostics helper: a short, readable case name for logging. Unwraps a Command<_>/Event<_> envelope to its payload (via IEnvelope), then a C# `union` to its active case (its generated `.Value`), and falls back to the type name — so F# DUs and plain payloads work too. Reflection-based; meant for logs, not hot paths.

member.name: M:FCQRS.CSharp.EventActions.PersistAll``1(``0[])
member.summary: Create a PersistAllEvents action: all events from this command persist as one journal AtomicWrite (all-or-nothing), with sequential versions. State updates and publishes happen only after the whole batch is durable.

member.name: M:FCQRS.CSharp.EventActions.Persist``1(``0)
member.summary: Create a PersistEvent action (event will be persisted and published)

member.name: M:FCQRS.CSharp.EventActions.Ignore``1
member.summary: Create an IgnoreEvent action (command is ignored, no event produced)

member.name: M:FCQRS.CSharp.EventActions.Defer``1(``0)
member.summary: Create a DeferEvent action (event is returned but not persisted - for errors/rejections)

member.name: T:FCQRS.CSharp.EventActions
member.summary: C#-friendly factory methods for EventAction

member.name: T:FCQRS.CSharp.StringTypes
member.summary: Obsolete alias — the bool/out Try-create overloads now live on <see cref="T:FCQRS.CSharp.Values"/>.

member.name: T:FCQRS.CSharp.Results
member.summary: Obsolete alias for <see cref="T:FCQRS.CSharp.FSharpResults"/>. Renamed off the generic 'Results' (which also collides with Microsoft.AspNetCore.Http.Results).

member.name: M:FCQRS.CSharp.FSharpResults.Ok``2(``0)
member.summary: Create a successful result

member.name: M:FCQRS.CSharp.FSharpResults.Error``2(``1)
member.summary: Create an error result

member.name: T:FCQRS.CSharp.FSharpResults
member.summary: C#-friendly factory methods for constructing an F# Result (Ok/Error) from C#.

member.name: T:FCQRS.CSharp.Helpers
member.summary: Obsolete alias for <see cref="T:FCQRS.CSharp.Values"/>. Renamed because these are factories for FCQRS's value/identifier types, not general-purpose helpers. Will be removed in a future preview.

member.name: M:FCQRS.CSharp.Values.TryCreateShortString(System.String,FCQRS.Model.Data.ShortString@)
member.summary: Try to create a ShortString, C# Try-pattern style: returns success + out value.

member.name: M:FCQRS.CSharp.Values.TryCreateShortString(System.String)
member.summary: Try to create a ShortString (returns Result instead of throwing)

member.name: M:FCQRS.CSharp.Values.TryCreateLongString(System.String,FCQRS.Model.Data.LongString@)
member.summary: Try to create a LongString, C# Try-pattern style: returns success + out value.

member.name: M:FCQRS.CSharp.Values.TryCreateLongString(System.String)
member.summary: Try to create a LongString (returns Result instead of throwing)

member.name: M:FCQRS.CSharp.Values.NewMessageId
member.summary: Create a new MessageId from a GUID v7

member.name: M:FCQRS.CSharp.Values.NewCID
member.summary: Create a new CID from a GUID v7

member.name: M:FCQRS.CSharp.Values.CreateVersion(System.Int64)
member.summary: Create a Version from a non-negative int64 (throws on failure)

member.name: M:FCQRS.CSharp.Values.CreateShortString(System.String)
member.summary: Create a ShortString from a string (throws on failure)

member.name: M:FCQRS.CSharp.Values.CreateMessageId(System.String)
member.summary: Create a MessageId from a string (throws on failure)

member.name: M:FCQRS.CSharp.Values.CreateLongString(System.String)
member.summary: Create a LongString from a string (throws on failure)

member.name: M:FCQRS.CSharp.Values.CreateCID(System.String)
member.summary: Create a CID from a string (throws on failure)

member.name: M:FCQRS.CSharp.Values.CreateAggregateId(System.String)
member.summary: Create an AggregateId from a string (throws on failure)

member.name: T:FCQRS.CSharp.Values
member.summary: Factory methods for creating FCQRS's strongly-typed value/identifier types (CID, AggregateId, MessageId, ShortString, LongString, Version) from C#.

member.name: T:FCQRS.CSharp.AsyncExtensions
member.summary: Obsolete: nested in a module, so its [<Extension>] AsTask was never discoverable as an extension from C# (hence the duplicate ToTask). Use the top-level `AsTask` extension instead: `using FCQRS;` then `someAsync.AsTask()`.

member.name: T:FCQRS.CSharp.AggregateRefs`2
member.summary: The two C#-facing pieces of a wired aggregate: a Factory for entity refs (used by sagas to target the aggregate) and a Handler to send a command and await its event (used by the delivery layer). Returned by InitAggregate.

member.name: T:FCQRS.CSharp.AggregateFactory
member.summary: C# delegate for an aggregate's entity-ref factory: an id -> its sharded actor ref. sp.AggregateFactory<T>() returns one; sagas use it to target an aggregate.

member.name: T:FCQRS.CSharp.Handler`2
member.summary: C# delegate for command handlers - returns Task<Event<TEvent>>

member.name: T:FCQRS.CSharp
member.summary: C# interoperability helpers for FCQRS Provides simpler APIs for consuming FCQRS from C#
